stories.headline: Jeremiyah Love Stakes His Claim in the Desert
stories.read: Hot off the wire, pardner: the Cardinals handed rookie Jeremiyah Love the No. 3 pick and look fixin' to hand him the reins right quick. The chatter's got him as a high-end No. 2 back come the land grab, end of the second or top of the third -- and a rookie that touches the ball that much pays the rent regardless of polish. Watch the money patch close to paydirt, 'cause Conner and Allgeier might steal a goal-line carry or two early. But this here's a vein worth strikin' before the saloon prices him into Round 1.

stories.headline: DeVonta Smith Inherits the Whole Philly Creek
stories.read: With A.J. Brown shipped east, ol' Batman is the lone No. 1 on the range in Philadelphia. Brown left behind a near-28-percent cut of the throws and a mountain of air yards, and most of that haul rides straight to Smith -- folks already nudgin' his stock up toward the WR1 mix. The man's been a locked-in stud every time Brown's been laid up, so the volume ain't a guess. Go pan that creek now while the saloon still has him slotted as a mid-round WR2.

stories.headline: Two Tight Ends, One Saddle -- Giants Room Gets Crowded
stories.read: John Harbaugh went and handed Isaiah Likely forty million to ride alongside Theo Johnson, and now he's braggin' there ain't a better tight end room in the league. That's mighty fine for the Giants -- and mighty poor for your lineup, pardner. When two good critters split the same haul, neither one carves out a steady week-to-week stake. Don't go reachin' for Johnson's breakout on that crowded ranch; let some other fella pay full price for the headache.
